back_translation: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan, in the wilderness, in the Arabah opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
Eleven days from Horeb by the way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.
And it was in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first of the month, Moses spoke to the sons of Israel according to all that the Lord commanded him to them.
After he struck down Sihon king of the Amorites who dwelt in Heshbon, and Og king of Bashan who dwelt in Ashtaroth at Edrei.
Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses began to explain this Torah, saying:
The Lord our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying: You have stayed long enough at this mountain.

Turn and journey, and come to the hill country of the Amorites and to all its neighbors, in the Arabah, in the hill country, in the lowland, in the Negev, and along the seacoast—the land of the Canaanites and Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
See, I have placed before you the land. Enter and possess the land that the Lord swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their offspring after them.
And I spoke to you at that time, saying: I am not able alone to bear you.
The Lord your God has multiplied you, and behold, you are today like the stars of the sky in number.
May the Lord, the God of your fathers, add to you a thousand times as many as you are, and bless you as He has spoken to you.
How can I bear alone your troubles, your burdens, and your disputes?
Choose for yourselves wise, understanding, and known men according to your tribes, and I will place them as your heads.
And you answered me and said: Good is the word that you have spoken to do.

So I took the heads of your tribes, wise and known men, and I placed them as heads over you—commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officers for your tribes.
And I commanded your judges at that time, saying: Hear between your brothers, and judge righteousness between a man and his brother and his foreigner.

You shall not recognize faces in judgment; both small and great you shall hear. You shall not fear from before a man, for the judgment belongs to God. And the matter that is too hard for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.
And I commanded you at that time all the things that you should do.

And we set out from Horeb and went through all that great and terrible wilderness which you saw, by the way of the hill country of the Amorites, just as the Lord our God commanded us. And we came to Kadesh-barnea.
And I said to you: You have come to the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is giving us.
See, the Lord your God has placed before you the land. Go up, possess it, as the Lord, the God of your fathers, has spoken to you. Do not fear and do not be dismayed.

Then all of you approached me and said: Let us send men before us, and they will search out for us the land, and they will bring back word to us concerning the way we should go up and the cities we should enter.
And the word was good in my eyes, so I took from you twelve men, one man for each tribe.
And they turned and went up into the hill country, and came to the Valley of Eshcol, and spied it out.
And they took in their hand some of the fruit of the land and brought it down to us, and they brought back word to us and said: Good is the land that the Lord our God is giving to us.
But you were not willing to go up, and you rebelled against the mouth of the Lord your God.
And you grumbled in your tents and said: In the Lord's hatred of us, He has brought us out from the land of Egypt to give us into the hand of the Amorites to destroy us.

Where are we going up? Our brothers have melted our hearts, saying: The people are greater and taller than we, the cities are large and fortified up to the heavens, and we also saw the sons of the Anakim there.
And I said to you: Do not be terrified and do not fear them.
The Lord your God who goes before you, He will fight for you, according to all that He did with you in Egypt before your eyes.
And in the wilderness that you saw, where the Lord your God carried you as a man carries his son, in all the way that you walked until you came to this place.
And in this matter you are not believing in the Lord your God,
who goes before you on the way, to seek out for you a place to camp—in fire by night to show you in the way you should walk, and in the cloud by day.
And the Lord heard the sound of your words, and He was angry and swore, saying:
If a man among these men, this evil generation, shall see the good land that I swore to give to your fathers,
except Caleb son of Jephunneh—he will see it, and to him I will give the land on which he has trodden, and to his sons, because he fully followed after the Lord.
Also with me the Lord was angry on your account, saying: Also you shall not enter there.
Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you, he will enter there. Strengthen him, for he will give it as an inheritance to Israel.
And your little ones, of whom you said they would become a spoil, and your sons who do not know today good and evil—they will enter there, and to them I will give it, and they will possess it.
But you, turn and journey to the wilderness by the way of the Sea of Reeds.

And you answered and said to me: We have sinned against the Lord. We will go up and fight, according to all that the Lord our God commanded us. And each of you girded on his weapons of war, and you thought it easy to go up to the hill country.
And the Lord said to me: Say to them: You shall not go up and you shall not fight, for I am not in your midst, lest you be struck down before your enemies.
And I spoke to you, and you did not listen, and you rebelled against the mouth of the Lord, and you presumptuously went up to the hill country.
And the Amorites who dwelt in that hill country came out to meet you, and they pursued you as the bees do, and they struck you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
And you returned and wept before the Lord, but the Lord did not listen to your voice, and He did not give ear to you.
And you dwelt in Kadesh many days, as the days that you dwelt.
